title: Single-source the EPI vintage — bronze-to-silver still hardcodes bronze_epi2024results while both ends now derive the year
description: task-028 parameterized the EPI table-name year at two of three sites: bronze_ingest_epi derives bronze_epi{p_epi_year}results and silver-to-gold2 derives silver_epi{EPI_YEAR}results from a module constant. bronze-to-silver.Notebook (lines 74/108) still hardcodes bronze_epi2024results -> silver_epi2024results, and data_quality_checks.Notebook hardcodes the same names in its schema/rowcount contracts. So the year is parameterized at both ends and hardcoded in the middle: a genuine 2025 pull would land in bronze_epi2025results and be silently ignored by silver and by the DQ suite. The residual failure mode is safe (bumping EPI_YEAR fails loudly on a missing silver table) rather than the silent mislabeling task-028 fixed, which is why this is a follow-up rather than a defect in task-028's delivery. NOTE: spec_v1 § Data Sources #2 states 'Bronze table: bronze_epi2024results' / 'Silver table: silver_epi2024results' as FIXED names, so closing this properly needs a spec reconciliation through /iterate — state the naming convention (bronze_epi{year}results) plus the single-vintage caveat, and decide whether bronze-to-silver should be parameterized or whether single-vintage is the intended design.
acceptanceCriteria: The EPI vintage has exactly one source of truth across bronze_ingest_epi, bronze-to-silver, silver-to-gold2 and data_quality_checks — no notebook hardcodes a year literal in an EPI table name.

A wrong-vintage run fails loudly rather than silently reading a stale table: reading a non-existent silver_epi{year}results raises, and the DQ suite's schema/rowcount contracts follow the same parameter.

spec_v1 § Data Sources #2 is reconciled through /iterate — it states the naming convention rather than the 2024 instance, and records whether multi-vintage is supported or single-vintage is the deliberate design.
[Erik/Fabric] a run with the current vintage still produces identical fact_epi_score output (this is a refactor, not a behavior change).
notes: CREATED 2026-07-23 by /work from task-028's verify-agent friction FR-021 and implement-agent issues_discovered. Not a defect in task-028 — that task's AC6 scoped only bronze_ingest_epi and silver-to-gold2, both of which it met.
Blocked-ish on a spec decision: parameterizing bronze-to-silver may be the wrong answer if single-vintage EPI is deliberate. Resolve that in /iterate before writing code.

FR-021 RESOLVED 2026-07-23 by /iterate — the SPEC half is done: § Data Sources #2 now documents the bronze_epi{year}results / silver_epi{year}results convention, names p_epi_year and EPI_YEAR as the deriving parameters, and carries the single-vintage caveat. This task's remaining scope is the CODE half: parameterize bronze-to-silver.Notebook (hardcodes both names at lines 74/108). ADDED SCOPE found while grounding the spec edit — data_quality_checks.Notebook's BLOCKING_CHECKS contains a hardcoded ('schema_validation','oem_lh.silver_epi2024results') entry, and per that set's own comment a stale entry does NOT error, it silently demotes the check to advisory. Parameterizing the notebooks without fixing that entry would quietly drop a blocking check.
